code_blocks.code:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 86400 * 14, "SECONDS")
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter lowercase(action_process_image_name) in ("wsl.exe", "wslhost.exe")
| filter lowercase(action_process_image_command_line) contains "curl"
    or lowercase(action_process_image_command_line) contains "wget"
    or lowercase(action_process_image_command_line) contains "socat"
    or lowercase(action_process_image_command_line) contains "/bin/bash -c"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username,
    causality_actor_process_image_name, action_process_image_command_line
| limit 200

scenario: WSL used to launch a Linux shell with network tooling.
snippet:
Scenario: imagine an operator using the Windows Subsystem for Linux to run
familiar Linux network or transfer tools outside the visibility of
Windows-only controls. WSL invocations with `curl`, `wget`, `bash`, or
`socat` in their arguments are worth a glance.

- https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1059/004/
- https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/basic-commands

Logic: not every WSL hit is bad; many developers live in WSL. The value is
in the join of WSL plus network or transfer tooling, especially on non-developer hosts.
